$comment: third-party NuGet package licenses. Maps SPDX-style ids to whether the
license is permissive enough for v2's posture per audit-checklist-2026 SEC-DEP-3.
Conservative posture: anything not on this list is flagged. The allowlist is
deliberately narrow to force an explicit operator decision on edge cases
rather than silently admitting unknown licenses.
How to add a license: append below, AND add a one-sentence rationale in the
'rationale' map. Bump the version. Commit message MUST link the PR / issue
Out-of-allowlist examples (intentionally not allowed): GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0,
AGPL-3.0, LGPL-2.1, LGPL-3.0, MPL-1.1, MPL-2.0 (case-by-case), CC-BY-NC-*,

alias_normalisations.$comment: Map free-form license-strings observed in NuGet metadata onto canonical SPDX ids.

rationale.MIT: Permissive; commercial-use OK; no copyleft; default for .NET ecosystem.
rationale.Apache-2.0: Permissive; explicit patent grant; commercial-use OK; widely used by Microsoft / Google libraries.
rationale.BSD-3-Clause: Permissive; commercial-use OK; common for academic-origin libraries.
rationale.BSD-2-Clause: Permissive; commercial-use OK; minimal restrictions vs BSD-3.
rationale.ISC: Permissive; equivalent to BSD-2/MIT in effect.
rationale.MS-PL: Microsoft Public License; permissive for commercial use; used by some legacy MS NuGets.
rationale.MS-EULA: Microsoft .NET Library license terms; covers framework / first-party Microsoft NuGets like System.* and Microsoft.* packages.
